signals.title: AUD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + RBA Outlook + CESI
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7015. The Australian Dollar is the weakest G10 currency this week, averaging -1.111% against peers, while the USD is the strongest at +0.758%. This 1.869% spread highlights a significant bilateral theme. NAB forecasts for future RBA rate cuts are weighing on AUD, despite the RBA's current hiking stance (4.35%) and a +0.17% mildly restrictive real rate. Australian economic data is also disappointing, with a CESI net of -8 misses. This fundamental weakness is driving AUD/USD lower, with options market showing moderate put skew (AUD/USD 25d RR -1.15). Watch for RBA's next meeting on 16 Jun for any shift in forward guidance.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Intervention Risk + Carry + Positioning
signals.text: USD/JPY at 160.51, trading above the 160.00 level that has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings. Intervention risk is elevated on a sustained break higher. The pair is supported by a significant +290bp carry premium favoring USD (SOFR 3.63% vs TONA 0.73%). Speculative positioning shows a crowded JPY net short of -105,136 (long%=29.5%), making it vulnerable to a short-squeeze if the BoJ intervenes or shifts its hawkish bias (BoJ hike priced for 16 Jun). The current yield curve regime is a BEAR STEEPENER, which typically supports carry trades. Watch for any official BoJ comments or price action above 160.50.

signals.title: GBP/USD — CPI Reaction + Volatility Premium + Carry
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3382, rising after US May CPI matched estimates, which weighed on the USD. The pair benefits from a -10bp carry premium favoring GBP (SONIA 3.73% vs SOFR 3.63%). Options market shows an elevated volatility risk premium (GBP/USD IV 11.0% vs HV30 6.5%, VRP +4.5%), indicating strong hedging demand for GBP upside. However, 25d RR at -0.81 shows a moderate put skew, suggesting options traders are still hedging against GBP weakness despite the recent rally. Watch for the next BoE meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 18 Jun for further directional cues.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs Retail Divergence + Carry
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5813. Speculative positioning shows a crowded NZD net short of -27,797 (long%=15.1%), indicating institutional bearishness. However, retail sentiment is extremely long at 88% (vs 12% short), creating a significant divergence. This suggests retail traders are fading the institutional short, posing a contrarian reversal risk if the institutional short unwinds. The pair faces structural headwinds from a +150bp carry premium favoring USD (SOFR 3.63% vs OCR 2.25%). Watch for the next RBNZ meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 8 Jul for any shift in policy stance that is likely to trigger a short-squeeze.
